# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Target - Build, Test, Comment

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

concurrency:
  group: pr-target-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  test:
    if: ${{ !github.event.pull_request.draft }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR head (untrusted)
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
          fetch-depth: 0
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Set up Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        id: test
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -o pipefail
          npm test 2>&1 | tee test-output.txt

      - name: Comment results on PR
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');

            const outputPath = 'test-output.txt';
            let output = '';
            try { output = fs.readFileSync(outputPath, 'utf8'); } catch (e) { output = '(no test output captured)'; }

            const max = 60000;
            if (output.length > max) {
              output = output.slice(0, max) + '\n\n...(truncated)...';
            }

            const conclusion = `${{ job.status }}`;
            const sha = context.payload.pull_request.head.sha;
            const runUrl = `${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`;

            const body =
              `### CI results for \`${sha.slice(0, 7)}\`\n` +
              `**Status:** ${conclusion}\n\n` +
              `**Run:** ${runUrl}\n\n` +
              `<details><summary>Test output</summary>\n\n` +
              `\`\`\`\n${output}\n\`\`\`\n` +
              `</details>`;

            const issue_number = context.payload.pull_request.number;

            const marker = '<!-- pr-target-ci-results -->';
            const finalBody = `${marker}\n${body}`;

            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number,
              per_page: 100,
            });

            const existing = comments.find(c => c.user?.type === 'Bot' && c.body?.includes(marker));

            if (existing) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: existing.id,
                body: finalBody,
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number,
                body: finalBody,
              });
            }
